title: Dorande (species)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Dorande_aliens.png]] |name=Dorande |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Droids (1986) 5|''Droids'' (1986) 5]] |class=[[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Pink]] |haircolor=Black |feathers= |eyecolor=Yellow |distinctions=*Pointy-[[Ear/Legends|eared]] *Bald |lifespan= |origin=[[Dorande]] |habitat= |diet= |language=*[[Dorande language]]In [[Droids (1986) 5|''Droids'' (1986) 5]], the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] was charged with [[Translator droid/Legends|translating]] a written proposal presented by the Dorande [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]], indicating that it was written in a [[Language/Legends|language]] other than [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]. *[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] }} {{Quote|With the element of surprise, the planet will be ours… In the name of Dorande, of course!|Ambassador Kawakal, to Baron Starlock|}} '''Dorande''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of pointed-[[Ear/Legends|eared]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dorande]]. At the start of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Dark Times]], they attempted to seize the world of [[R-Duba]], conspiring with the planet's royal advisors to [[Death/Legends|murder]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Jagoda]]. While the Dorandes intended to raid Jagoda's palace, their plans were foiled by [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[Kirk Windjammer]]. ==Biology and appearance== [[File:Dorande_conspirator.png|thumb|left|150px|A Dorande (right) with a Human (left)]] The Dorande were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] configuration: like the [[Human/Legends|Humans]], they had a single head, a torso and walked on two [[Limb/Legends|legs]]. Two arms extended from their shoulders and ended in five-fingered hands that included an opposable thumb. Their faces sported two forward-facing [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a single [[Nose/Legends|nose]], and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] filled with white, blunt [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. What truly distinguished Dorandes from Humans were their [[Egg/Legends|egg]]-shaped cranium, their [[Color/Legends|pink]] skin and their tapered [[Ear/Legends|ears]] with long, dangling lobes. While entirely bald, the Dorandes were not completely hairless, as several individuals displayed black brows above their yellow eyes. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|Shall we raise the flag, Commander?''"
"''Yes! Victory for Dorande!|A Dorande officer and his commander|}} In the early years of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Dark Times]], the Dorandes commonly spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], the most prominent [[Language/Legends|language]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], although they still had a [[Dorande language|tongue]] of their own. In addition to speaking Basic, they also used the [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic alphabet]]. They were a bellicose people who maintained a fleet of [[Gunship/Legends|convertible gunships]] and would not hesitate to capture a planet for the glory of their own culture. ==History== {{Quote|Commander, sensors show about twenty ships approaching!''"
"''What?|A Dorande officer and his commander|}} The Dorandes evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Dorande|same name]], a world that was located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' At the start of the Dark Times, [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Fez Kawakal]] represented Dorande at the court of [[Jagoda]], the reigning [[Prince/Legends|prince]] of the planet [[R-Duba]]. Under the pretense of bringing a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] proposal to the prince, Kawakal had come to conspire with one of his advisors, [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Gavern Starlock]]. The two planned to have the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[BX-00]] [[Death/Legends|murder]] the prince so Dorande could invade R-Duba. The [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who had overheard the conversation, saved the prince's life and told him the truth. As a consequence, Jagoda banished Starlock and Kawakal from his planet, appointing C-3PO his chief advisor. But the Dorandes' invasion plans were not completely foiled. Meanwhile, the young [[Kirk Windjammer]], captain of an [[anti-grav loader]] christened ''[[Seaskimmer]]'', accepted a strange job from a mysterious figure he met at the [[Red Asteroid Bar]]. He would have to load his craft with cargo, and then drop it in the middle of the sea. When he reached the arranged drop-off point, Windjammer encountered a fleet of ships. When it appeared that the cargo crates contained illegal [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], Windjammer tried to escape his clients, who removed their Human masks and revealed their Dorande origins. The Dorandes, whose vessel was in fact a gunship, pursued the young man, and ended up destroying the ''Seaskimmer''. Unbeknownst to them, Windjammer and his droid had escaped in an underwater [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. The two then reached Jagoda's palace, where they could warn the prince Jagoda of the impending invasion. Prince Jagoda charged Windjammer with leading a defense force of forty heavily armed [[Gunship/Legends|R-Duba gunships]] against the enemy. Since his fleet was unprepared for a coordinated attack, the Dorande commander ordered a retreat. ==Behind the scenes== When clarification on the pointy-eared [[Valenthyne Farfalla]] was sought from [[Leland Chee]], keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]], he shared his personal theory that "all pointy-eared species are either [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] or closely related to Sephi,"{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1395#16675997|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-06-08|time=6:48 AM}} a reference to a pointy-eared species from ''[[Jedi: Yoda]]'', published in [[2004]].''[[Jedi: Yoda]]'' ==Appearances== *[[Droids (1986) 5|''Droids'' (1986) 5]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|I||Dorande}} {{1stID|Dorande}} *{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Dorande }} [[Category:Outer Rim Territories sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Dorandes]] }}
